title: Nehemiah hears Jerusalem's distress
summary_text: Nehemiah hears that Jerusalem's wall is broken and its people are shamed, and he responds with fasting and prayer. He confesses Israel's sin and appeals to the covenant promises given through Moses. The chapter begins the book with grief that turns into purposeful dependence on God.
key_points: News of Jerusalem's condition burdens Nehemiah.
He fasts and prays before acting.
He confesses Israel's covenant failure.
He asks God for favor with the king.
evidence_phrases: the wall of Jerusalem is broken down

title: Nehemiah inspects and begins rebuilding
summary_text: Nehemiah receives permission from Artaxerxes and travels to Jerusalem with royal support. After a quiet inspection of the ruined walls, he gathers the leaders and calls them to rebuild. The chapter shifts from lament to action as opposition is already starting to take shape.
key_points: Nehemiah gains permission to go to Jerusalem.
He inspects the wall at night.
He calls the leaders to rebuild.
Enemy mockery begins immediately.
evidence_phrases: the good hand of my God
let us rise up and build

title: The wall is rebuilt section by section
summary_text: Families, priests, goldsmiths, merchants, and city officials repair the wall at many different points. The chapter is a roll call of shared labor, where each group works on its own section and the whole city benefits. It records restoration as a communal task rather than a single heroic act.
key_points: Different groups repair different sections.
Priests and workers labor together.
The wall rises through collective effort.
The chapter honors many specific families.

title: Opposition, prayer, and armed labor
summary_text: Sanballat and Tobiah mock the rebuilding, but Nehemiah answers with prayer and determined work. As threats increase, the people labor with a sword in one hand and a trowel in the other. The chapter shows that faithful rebuilding often requires both prayer and vigilance.
key_points: Enemies ridicule the rebuilding effort.
Nehemiah prays instead of stopping.
Builders keep working with weapons nearby.
The wall progresses despite threats.
evidence_phrases: our God will fight for us

title: Nehemiah confronts injustice among the people
summary_text: Nehemiah learns that the poor are being oppressed by debt, interest, and the loss of family land. He confronts the nobles, calls for repayment, and models generosity by refusing the governor's extra food allowance. The chapter shows that rebuilding a city also means repairing its justice.
key_points: The poor are being exploited by creditors.
Nehemiah confronts the nobles directly.
He restores what was taken unjustly.
He refuses to burden the people himself.

title: The wall is completed despite schemes
summary_text: Nehemiah's enemies try intimidation, false reports, and religious pretense to stop the project. He refuses the traps, and the wall is finished in a remarkable fifty-two days. The chapter credits the success to God and reveals how opposition can unintentionally magnify the work.
key_points: The enemies shift from mockery to schemes.
Nehemiah refuses false invitations.
The wall is completed quickly.
The people see God's hand in the result.
evidence_phrases: I am doing a great work
they perceived that this work was wrought of our God

title: The city is secured and the return list is found
summary_text: Nehemiah appoints gatekeepers, singers, and guards for Jerusalem, then discovers the genealogical register of the first returnees. The long list confirms the restored community's legitimate roots and helps order the city. The chapter turns from building walls to building a stable civic life.
key_points: Jerusalem's gates are organized for safety.
A genealogy of returnees is found.
The city receives an ordered population.
The record links the present to the first return.

title: Ezra reads the law and the people keep booths
summary_text: Ezra reads the law aloud to the assembled people, and the Levites help explain it so everyone can understand. The hearing of God's word leads directly to repentance, joy, and the feast of booths. The chapter shows that the restored city is rebuilt around Scripture and obedience.
key_points: The law is read before the assembly.
The Levites explain the meaning.
The people grieve and then rejoice.
The feast of booths is kept faithfully.

title: National confession and covenant history
summary_text: The people gather in fasting and confession, recounting God's faithfulness from creation through the exodus and exile. Their prayer is long because it holds together mercy, rebellion, judgment, and hope. The chapter turns history into worship by naming both God's patience and Israel's repeated failure.
key_points: The people fast and confess their sins.
Israel's history is rehearsed before God.
Mercy and rebellion are both named.
The prayer leads toward covenant renewal.
evidence_phrases: stand up and bless the LORD
you are a God ready to pardon
many years did you bear with them

title: The people seal the covenant
summary_text: The leaders, Levites, and priests sign a covenant to walk in God's law, keep the Sabbath, and support the temple. The commitments are practical and specific, showing that repentance must become public obedience. The chapter moves confession into binding promises.
key_points: The people bind themselves by covenant.
The Sabbath is explicitly addressed.
Temple support is organized.
The signers commit to concrete obedience.
evidence_phrases: enter into a curse and into an oath

title: Jerusalem is repopulated
summary_text: Lots are cast to bring a tenth of the people to Jerusalem, and volunteers settle there as well. The chapter lists the residents, priests, Levites, and officials who now live in the city. Rebuilding is no longer only about walls but also about filling the city with covenant life.
key_points: A tenth of the people move into Jerusalem.
Volunteers settle in the city.
Residents and officials are listed.
Jerusalem becomes populated again.
evidence_phrases: the people blessed all the men

title: Priestly lists and the wall dedication
summary_text: The chapter lists priests and Levites across generations and then tells of the joyful dedication of the wall. Two great choirs process in opposite directions until they meet in praise, and the celebration becomes loud and communal. The restored city now has both memory and worship on display.
key_points: Priestly and Levitical lines are listed.
The wall is dedicated with song.
Two thanksgiving processions circle the city.
Joy marks the completed restoration.

title: Final reforms against compromise
summary_text: Nehemiah returns to Jerusalem and finds several covenant breaches: Eliashib's compromise with Tobiah, neglected Levites, Sabbath trade, and mixed marriages. He responds with decisive reforms, cleansing the temple, restoring support, and confronting those who profaned the city. The chapter ends the book with sharp, practical obedience.
key_points: Tobiah is removed from the temple area.
The Levites are restored to their duties.
Sabbath trading is shut down.
Mixed marriages are confronted again.
evidence_phrases: the chamber of the house of God
